jobs.title: Futures User Operations 合约专业用户运营
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget is the world's largest Universal Exchange (UEX), serving over 125 million users and offering access to over 2M crypto tokens, 100+ tokenized stocks, ETFs, commodities, FX, and precious metals such as gold. The ecosystem is committed to helping users trade smarter with its AI agent, which co-pilots trade execution.

Bitget is driving crypto adoption through strategic partnerships with LALIGA and MotoGP™. Aligned with its global impact strategy, Bitget has joined hands with UNICEF to support blockchain education for 1.1 million people by 2027. Bitget currently leads in the tokenized TradFi market, providing the industry's lowest fees and highest liquidity across 150 regions worldwide.

What you'll do

 Trading Experience Enhancement：
Conduct regular competitor research and user interviews; segment retail users and professional traders, and drive the implementation of solutions addressing core pain points and trading experience requirements.
Futures Product Operations：
Collaborate with the Product team to research competitor products and internal product user journeys; identify product gaps and drive implementation to enhance product competitiveness.
TradFi Futures Operations：
Identify market hotspots across TradFi assets, including precious metals, commodities, and U.S. equities; collaborate on the listing and delisting evaluation of TradFi futures/contracts, and support promotional campaigns around trending market opportunities.

What you'll need

More than 3
requirements: More than 3 years of experience in operations or product roles at mainstream exchanges or traditional securities brokerage firms.
Deep understanding of futures/derivatives trading logic, with knowledge of professional futures traders’ user profiles and needs.
Strong understanding of TradFi assets, including precious metals, commodities, and equities.
Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to identify issues from trading data and drive decision-making.
Fluent in spoken English and able to use English as a working language.
responsibilities: Trading Experience Enhancement：

Conduct regular competitor research and user interviews; segment retail users and professional traders, and drive the implementation of solutions addressing core pain points and trading experience requirements.
Futures Product Operations：
Collaborate with the Product team to research competitor products and internal product user journeys; identify product gaps and drive implementation to enhance product competitiveness.
TradFi Futures Operations：

Identify market hotspots across TradFi assets, including precious metals, commodities, and U.S. equities; collaborate on the listing and delisting evaluation of TradFi futures/contracts, and support promotional campaigns around trending market opportunities.

jobs.title: Financial Risk Control Product Manager 金融风控产品（专家）
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget is the world's largest Universal Exchange (UEX), serving over 125 million users and offering access to over 2M crypto tokens, 100+ tokenized stocks, ETFs, commodities, FX, and precious metals such as gold. The ecosystem is committed to helping users trade smarter with its AI agent, which co-pilots trade execution.

Bitget is driving crypto adoption through strategic partnerships with LALIGA and MotoGP™. Aligned with its global impact strategy, Bitget has joined hands with UNICEF to support blockchain education for 1.1 million people by 2027. Bitget currently leads in the tokenized TradFi market, providing the industry's lowest fees and highest liquidity across 150 regions worldwide.

What you'll do

Responsible for the daily management and optimization of the exchange’s financial parameters. Use quantitative analysis to drive refined adjustments of core parameters such as margin requirements, leverage, and limits, improving user experience and platform competitiveness while keeping risks under control.
 Conduct daily inspections of platform-wide parameters, including margin rates, maintenance margin rates, maximum leverage, position limits, and funding rates. Provide optimization recommendations based on market changes and drive implementation.
 Regularly analyze liquidation data to identify trading pairs and user segments with high risk of negative equity / socialized loss. Deliver improvement proposals, such as increasing margin requirements, reducing lever
requirements: More than 5 years of finance-related experience, including at least 3 years of risk control or trading experience at a centralized cryptocurrency exchange (CEX).
Trader experience is preferred. Candidates should have personal trading experience in crypto or traditional financial markets, either manually or quantitatively.
Familiar with the risk characteristics of cryptocurrency derivatives, including perpetual futures, delivery futures, and options, as well as the operating mechanisms of exchanges.
Experience in the risk control team of a top 3 major cryptocurrency exchange is preferred.
responsibilities: Responsible for the daily management and optimization of the exchange’s financial parameters. Use quantitative analysis to drive refined adjustments of core parameters such as margin requirements, leverage, and limits, improving user experience and platform competitiveness while keeping risks under control.

Conduct daily inspections of platform-wide parameters, including margin rates, maintenance margin rates, maximum leverage, position limits, and funding rates. Provide optimization recommendations based on market changes and drive implementation.

Regularly analyze liquidation data to identify trading pairs and user segments with high risk of negative equity / socialized loss. Deliver improvement proposals, such as increasing margin requirements, reducing leverage, or limiting positions.

Build a dynamic parameter adjustment model based on multi-dimensional indicators such as market volatility, liquidity depth, and position concentration, enabling automated or semi-automated parameter tuning.

jobs.title: Financial Product Manager  金融产品经理（专家）
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget is the world's largest Universal Exchange (UEX), serving over 125 million users and offering access to over 2M crypto tokens, 100+ tokenized stocks, ETFs, commodities, FX, and precious metals such as gold. The ecosystem is committed to helping users trade smarter with its AI agent, which co-pilots trade execution.

Bitget is driving crypto adoption through strategic partnerships with LALIGA and MotoGP™. Aligned with its global impact strategy, Bitget has joined hands with UNICEF to support blockchain education for 1.1 million people by 2027. Bitget currently leads in the tokenized TradFi market, providing the industry's lowest fees and highest liquidity across 150 regions worldwide.

What you'll do

Responsible for the product design of the core underlying trading risk-control mechanisms of the exchange. Lead the rule design, iteration, and optimization of key modules, including the margin model, liquidation engine, Auto-Deleveraging (ADL), and insurance fund.
 The business scope covers traditional cryptocurrency futures/contracts as well as new products such as Stock Tokens and stock perpetual contracts. The role requires a strong understanding of the underlying mechanisms of derivatives and the ability to translate them into executable product solutions, including PRDs, rule tables, and acceptance criteria. Work closely with quantitative, risk control, and engineering teams to turn risk models from formulas into productized solutions.

What you'll need

Mor
requirements: More than 5 years of experience as a financial product manager, including at least 3 years of experience in trading product design at a centralized cryptocurrency exchange (CEX). Experience with futures/contracts or derivatives products is required.

Deep understanding of trading risk-control mechanisms, with the ability to clearly explain the complete logic flow of the three major modules: margin, liquidation, and ADL. This means not merely being familiar with these concepts, but being able to draw state machines and identify design pitfalls in edge cases.
Strong understanding of trading, with personal trading experience in the crypto market, particularly in futures/contracts and/or margin trading.
Experience at a top 3 major cryptocurrency exchange is preferred.
responsibilities: Responsible for the product design of the core underlying trading risk-control mechanisms of the exchange. Lead the rule design, iteration, and optimization of key modules, including the margin model, liquidation engine, Auto-Deleveraging (ADL), and insurance fund.

Assist in the development and implementation of company policies and procedures to ensure compliance with MiFID II / MiFIR requirements and other relevant regulatory obligations.

Monitor and supervise compliance with MiFID II / MiFIR obligations, conduct of business rules, and the requirements of national and European supervisory authorities (e.g. BaFin, ESMA).
Monitor and interpret relevant MiFID II / MiFIR laws, regulations, and regulatory guidance, advising on their impact on the company's operations and business strategies.
Conduct risk assessments and reviews of implemented MiFID II policies, controls, and fra
requirements: University Degree in Law, Economics, Finance, or a related field, with a preference for candidates with a legal background.

Strong knowledge of MiFID II / MiFIR requirements, including client categorisation, suitability and appropriateness, best execution, product governance, transaction reporting, and conduct of business rules.

Familiarity with ESMA guidelines and the broader European regulatory landscape for investment services, with an awareness of how emerging frameworks such as MiCA may interact with MiFID II obligations.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage effectively with regulators, senior management, and cross-functional teams.
Good command of written and oral English; proficiency in German is strongly preferred but is not a knock-out criterion.

responsibilities: Assist in the development and implementation of company policies and procedures to ensure compliance with MiFID II / MiFIR requirements and other relevant regulatory obligations.

jobs.title: CFD量化风控专家（HK/MY）
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget is one of the world's leading digital assets ecosystems. With over 120 million registered users, Bitget has one of the most comprehensive suites of blockchain products and services available via bitget.com.

Our mission is to support the growth of the digital assets industry and we believe it represents the future of finance. What we do empowers the future of finance by ensuring secure, efficient and smart digital transactions.

We are one of the fastest growing companies in the digital asset sector. If you are looking for cutting-edge work, where you will have opportunities to develop your career among peers who are experts in their field, and you believe in the future of digital currency, then look no further than Bitget!

What you'll do

1. User Profiling Feature Engineering & Quantitative Client Segmentation Modeling
Feature Mining: Extract and construct a quantitative feature library reflecting clients' trading habits, risk exposures, and profitability from massive, high-frequency Tick data and user behavior data (e.g., capital utilization rates, overnight position preferences, dynamic win rates, Sharpe ratios, slippage sensitivity, etc.).
Effectiveness Evaluation & Selection: Establish a rigorous feature validation framework (e.g., IV/WOE analysis, T-tests/hypothesis testing, correlation analysis, SHAP values for feature importance, etc.) to eliminate noisy factors.
Anti-Overfitting & Data Leakage Prevention: Ensure the decay rate of features in out-of-
requirements: 1. Educational Background

Bachelor’s degree or above from a top-tier university (985/211 or equivalent global ranking); major in Mathematics, Statistics, Financial Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, or related quantitative/STEM disciplines.
2. Professional Experience

1–3 years of experience in quantitative risk control, algorithmic trading, or data science within the financial industry; backgrounds in Forex or CFD derivatives trading platforms are highly preferred.
3. Core Skills
Technical Proficiency: Expert-level command of Python (including Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow/PyTorch, etc.) and SQL.
Analytical Foundation: Solid expertise in machine learning, statistical analysis, and financial time-series analysis.
4. Business Acumen
responsibilities: 1. User Profiling Feature Engineering & Quantitative Client Segmentation Modeling

Effectiveness Evaluation & Selection: Establish a rigorous feature validation framework (e.g., IV/WOE analysis, T-tests/hypothesis testing, correlation analysis, SHAP values for feature importance, etc.) to eliminate noisy factors.

Anti-Overfitting & Data Leakage Prevention: Ensure the decay rate of features in out-of-sample and live trading environments remains controllable; strictly prevent look-ahead bias (future functions) to guarantee model robustness and generalizability.

Client Segmentation & Flow Routing: Build machine learning or statistical models to accurately identify "toxic flow" versus standard retail flow. Provide quantitative decision support for the A/B Book hedging and routing engine to maximize revenue and minimize risk.
2. Risk Control Algorithm Design & Hedging Strategies

Dynamic Margin & Liquidation Algorithms: Optimize the platform's dynamic leverage rules and tiered maintenance margin models to effectively mitigate negative equity/runaway loss risks under extreme market conditions.

Risk Exposure & VaR Models: Establish a real-time risk exposure monitoring system for multi-asset classes (Forex, Commodities, Indices, and other CFDs); develop quantitative models for VaR (Value at Risk) and stress testing.

Anomalous Trading & Fraud Detection: Design anti-abuse/anti-cheating algorithms to identify and block illicit or disruptive trading behaviors in real time, such as latency arbitrage, high-frequency scalping, and market manipulation.

jobs.title: 量化研究员-高频做市方向
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget is the world's largest Universal Exchange (UEX), serving over 125 million users and offering access to over 2M crypto tokens, 100+ tokenized stocks, ETFs, commodities, FX, and precious metals such as gold. The ecosystem is committed to helping users trade smarter with its AI agent, which co-pilots trade execution.

Bitget is driving crypto adoption through strategic partnerships with LALIGA and MotoGP™. Aligned with its global impact strategy, Bitget has joined hands with UNICEF to support blockchain education for 1.1 million people by 2027. Bitget currently leads in the tokenized TradFi market, providing the industry's lowest fees and highest liquidity across 150 regions worldwide.

岗位职责：
策略开发与优化
设计、回测并部署自动化交易模型，包括定价引擎、波动率管理和库存控制。
研究市场微观结构，挖掘 alpha 信号，改进现有算法（如价差捕获、价差套利、资金费率套利）。
将研究见解转化为交易信号，并通过回测和实盘模拟进行验证。
交易与风险管理
管理日常交易活动：持续报出买卖价差，监控风险限额，调整跨不同交易所的碎片化库存规模。
实施稳健的风险控制措施（PnL 归因、回撤限制、对冲风险敞口） ，并适应 7×24 小时的市场环境。
分析交易后的盈亏驱动因素、市场冲击和滑点，以优化定价模型。
技术与协作
与量化研究员、软件工程师及其他交易员密切合作，改进交易系统和降低执行延迟。
参与低延迟架构设计与优化（例如多线程、分布式系统）。
持续跟踪加密货币市场动态、交易所 API 更新以及 DeFi 协议，发掘新的交易机会。

任职要求：
本科及以上学历，具备3-5年支付行业或相关领域工作经验。
对法币系统及相关知识有深入理解。
熟悉法币数据结构与框架，具备扎实的数据处理能力。
具备独立的数据分析能力，能够从数据中提炼出有价值的洞察。
有竞品公司（Web3、交易或金融/银行/支付行业）相同职位经验者优先。

jobs.title: RWA 股票风控策略分析师
jobs.description:
岗位职责
负责 CEX 平台 RWA 股票类产品的风险监控与策略分析，包括价格波动、流动性、敞口、持仓集中度及异常交易行为。
制定和优化股票类 RWA 产品风控规则，包括交易限额、持仓上限、价格偏离、熔断、强平、保证金及预警机制。
监控用户及机构客户交易行为，识别异常交易、市场操纵、对敲、自成交、刷量、价格拉抬打压等风险。
跟踪标的股票、ETF、指数及相关市场行情，评估链上/平台内 RWA 资产价格与外部市场价格偏离风险。
参与 RWA 股票产品上线前风险评估，包括标的流动性、波动率、交易时段、停牌、公司行为及监管风险。
进行风险数据分析，输出日报、周报、专题分析及风险事件复盘报告。
协同产品、交易、合规、技术团队，推动风控策略系统化、自动化和监控看板建设。
参与突发风险事件处理，如极端行情、价格异常、流动性枯竭、外部市场停牌或数据源异常。

任职要求
本科及以上学历，金融、经济、数学、统计、计算机、金融工程等相关专业优先。
2 年以上股票、券商、基金、量化交易、加密交易所或金融科技风控相关经验。
熟悉股票、ETF、指数等金融产品交易机制，了解美股、港股或 A 股市场规则者优先。
熟悉 CEX 交易机制、订单簿、撮合、保证金、强平、API 交易及异常交易监控逻辑者优先。
具备良好的数据分析能力，熟练使用 SQL、Excel，掌握 Python 者优先。
理解市场风险、流动性风险、价格偏离风险、信用风险及操作风险。
具备较强风险敏感度，能够独立分析异常交易行为并提出风控策略建议。
英文读写能力良好，能够跟踪海外股票市场、监管动态及公司公告。

As the Head of Institutional Department, you will view our exchange through the lens of a sophisticated trader. You will reverse-engineer our product offerings, fee models, and infrastructure to eliminate friction for HFT/prop desks, optimize liquidity, and aggressively scale Bitget’s institutional market share.

requirements: 1.Hard Experience & Background

This includes aggressive maker rebates (including negative fees), competitive taker structures, dynamic volume-based tiers, and capital efficiency mechanisms to maximize maker volume and platform net revenue.

Strategic Alignment: Bridge the gap between trading reality and exchange product development. Translate complex trading friction points (latency, capital efficiency, risk limits, order book dynamics) into actionable technical specifications for our product and engineering teams.

What you'll do

Responsible for technology infrastructure, cybersecurity, operational continuity, and custody/safekeeping of virtual assets.
This officer fulfills two governance purposes:
(a) it prevents the accumulation of incompatible functions in a single individual; and
(b) it directly addresses Article 2(IV) of Resolution 519 (“compatibility of the information technology infrastructure with the complexity and risks of the business”) by placing a specifically designated and technically qualified officer in charge of IT infrastructure.
Given the nature of Bitget's operations as a digital asset exchange, this function will likely receive focused attention from the BCB during pre-operational inspection (Art. 25 of Resolution 519).

What

Minimum of 7-10 years of progressive experience in technology leadership roles, including at least 3-5 years specifically in information security and 3-5 years in a CTO or similar role.
Deep understanding of information security principles, best practices, and relevant technologies (e.g., firewalls, intrusion detection systems, vulnerability management tools, cryptography, SIEM).
Expertise in software development, systems architecture, and IT infrastructure management.
Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, particularly in cross-cultural settings.
Availability to work flexible hours to accommodate communication with the Asia-based team.

jobs.description:
About us

Our mission is to support the growth of the digital assets industry and we believe it represents the future of finance. What we do empowers the future of finance by ensuring secure, efficient and smart digital transactions.

What you'll do

As a core quantitative researcher, you will directly participate in the entire lifecycle of the team's high-frequency trading system's strategy development, using data to extract the alpha (Alpha) of the market.
High-frequency factor mining: Conduct in-depth analysis of market data at massive levels (such as Level-2, Tick-level, or order book-intensive order streams), and apply statistical and machine learning methods for high-frequency feature engineering and factor mining.
Statistical modeling and strategy development: For specific long-tail assets, mainstream digital assets, or micro-market structures, build, test, and optimize high-frequency market-making (Market Making), statistical arbitrage (Stat Arb), or momentum/m
requirements: The first degree must be a bachelor's degree or above from a domestic 985 university (or an equivalent top-notch overseas university).

Professional background: Hardcore science and engineering majors such as mathematics, physics, statistics, computer science, engineering, etc., with strong mathematical and logical reasoning skills, as well as a solid foundation in probability theory and mathematical statistics.
High-frequency market-making strategy or domestic spot/futures Tick-level quantitative trading practical experience or research experience (mandatory).

Python expert: Proficiently using Python for data science work, proficiently mastering Polars (or Pandas), Numpy, Statsmodels, Scikit-learn, etc., and capable of efficiently processing and manipulating large-scale high-frequency time series data.

Factor and modeling experience: Familiar with the factor mining process, with a deep understanding or research interest in order book imbalance (OBI), realized volatility (Realized Volatility), or microstructure (Microstructure).

English proficiency: Possessing good academic literature reading skills in English, able to quickly convert cutting-edge quantitative papers (such as A-S market-making model variants, machine learning prediction, etc.) into code and conduct real trading tests.

Bonus points (not mandatory but have priority): Achieving excellent results in Kaggle, mathematical modeling competitions (MCM/ICM), top-level hackathons, or various algorithm/programming competitions (such as ICPC/CCPC).
responsibilities: As a core quantitative researcher, you will directly participate in the entire lifecycle of the team's high-frequency trading system's strategy development, using data to extract the alpha (Alpha) of the market.

High-frequency factor mining: Conduct in-depth analysis of market data at massive levels (such as Level-2, Tick-level, or order book-intensive order streams), and apply statistical and machine learning methods for high-frequency feature engineering and factor mining.

Statistical modeling and strategy development: For specific long-tail assets, mainstream digital assets, or micro-market structures, build, test, and optimize high-frequency market-making (Market Making), statistical arbitrage (Stat Arb), or momentum/mean reversion trading strategies.

Backtesting system and model validation: Write high-quality, rigorous high-frequency strategy backtesting scripts, conduct scientific validity tests, multicollinearity tests, and survival analyses on factors to ensure that the strategies are less prone to overfitting in live trading.
Inventory and risk management: Optimize the dynamic inventory control (Inventory Management), slippage assessment, and micro-hedging mechanisms of the strategies in live trading.

jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Manager / Lead
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget Wallet is the non-custodial crypto wallet designed to make crypto simple and secure for everyone. With over 80 million users, we bring together a full suite of crypto services, including swaps, market insights, staking, rewards, DApp exploration, and payment solutions. Bitget Wallet is experiencing rapid growth, and we are looking for a Talent Acquisition Lead to take full ownership of our talent strategy. This is a unique, high-impact opportunity to be the sole TA lead for the business unit, partnering directly with C-level leadership to define our hiring roadmap and build the world-class team that powers our next phase of expansion.

What you'll do
Talent Strategy & Ownership: Define and execute the hiring strategy for Bitget Wallet, aligning talent acquisition with core business objectives across all functions (Tech, Product, Ops, Marketing, and more).
C-Level Partnership: Work directly with executive leadership to forecast hiring needs, advise on talent market trends, and ensure our hiring plan supports long-term scalability.
End-to-End Execution: Own the full lifecycle of critical hires, from defining role requirements and sourcing strategy to final offers and onboarding. You will be the driving fo
requirements: Strategic Builder: You have 5+ years of TA experience, ideally in high-growth Tech, Web3, or Crypto environments. You know how to build hiring engines from the ground up.
Business-First Mindset: You don't just fill seats; you solve business problems. You understand product, tech, and operations well enough to advise leaders on talent trade-offs.
Executive Presence: You are comfortable partnering with C-level stakeholders, influencing decisions with data, and pushing back when necessary to ensure hiring quality.
Full-Stack Recruiter: You can strategize at the macro level but also execute at the micro level—sourcing, engaging, closing, and negotiating with top talent across diverse functions.
Data-Driven: You use metrics to drive efficiency, track pipeline health, and optimize conversion rates throughout the hiring funnel.
Global Vision: You have experience hiring across multiple regions and understand the nuances of building an international team.
If you are ambitious and believe that digital assets could be the next financial and technological revolution, please apply!
By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our Candidate Privacy Notice.

Design and iteration of trading risk control strategies: Participate in the construction of trading risk control strategies for DEX contracts, including designing a multi-dimensional risk control indicator system that covers user-level, pair-level, and platform-level risk control parameters, and establishing a dynamic parameter adjustment mechanism.
Management of platform ADL risk control mechanism: Participate in the design of DEX contract trading monitoring mechanisms, covering core indicators such as ADL trigger rate and insurance fund level, establish a multi-level early warning mechanism, and formulate gradient-triggered risk control monitoring plans; collaborate with the business product team to promote the implement
requirements: More than 5 years of experience in trading risk control, and 3 years of experience in trading risk control related to major exchanges or DeFi protocols. Prior experience in Perp DEX risk control is preferred.

responsibilities: Emergency response to unexpected events: Have the ability to promptly respond and troubleshoot DEX trading risk control incidents, covering extreme market conditions/failures, etc., and output improvement measures and promote their implementation.

requirements: 3–5 years of experience at a forex/CFD broker back-office trading desk (Dealing Desk), liquidity management, or risk control core position.

jobs.title: Senior KYC Specialist (Bitget EU)
jobs.description:
About us

Bitget is a global digital asset platform providing spot and derivatives trading and related products to retail and institutional clients. Bitget EU is committed to operating to high regulatory and compliance standards across Europe and to delivering a secure, fair, and transparent trading experience. We continuously strengthen our controls to prevent misuse of our platform and support a safe crypto ecosystem.

What you'll do

We are looking for an experienced Senior Due Diligence Specialist to join Bitget EU's Anti-Financial Crime (AFC) / Compliance team in Vienna. In this senior contributor role, you will perform and oversee complex KYC/EDD reviews for retail customers.
You will ensure all onboarding and periodic review processes are fully aligned with Austrian FM-GwG, WiEReG, EU AML/CFT requirements, sanctions expectations, and Transfer of Funds Regulation / Travel Rule considerations. This role is ideal for professionals with hands-on experience in KYC/EDD from crypto, fintech/EMI, or banking, who can apply a risk-based approach and produce high-quality audit-ready documentation.

Location: Vienna; Relocation from other EU countries possible

KYC / Retail Due Diligence
Conduct risk-based KYC/CDD/EDD/ODD reviews for EU retail customers in accordance with internal policies, FM-GwG requirements and group standards
Assess and document customer risk factors (jurisdiction, occupation, product usage, behavioural indicators, adverse information), including defining and
requirements: 5+ years of hands-on experience in KYC/CDD/EDD/ODD and/or KYB/corporate onboarding/AML-CFT roles, ideally in crypto, fintech/EMI, or banking
Solid working knowledge of Austrian FM-GwG and WiEReG, and familiarity with broader EU AML/CFT requirements and Transfer of Funds Regulation / Travel Rule considerations
Proven ability to assess SoF/SoW, analyse complex ownership structures and document clear, defensible rationales for approvals, restrictions and escalations
Experience with KYC tooling and data sources: ID&V solutions, commercial registries, beneficial ownership registers, screening systems, adverse media providers and case management platforms
Strong investigative writing and documentation skills; comfortable producing clear, audit-ready KYC rationales and escalation notes
Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal); German is a strong advantage for Vienna-based work
Highly detail-oriented, structured, independent and proactive, with the ability to manage multiple complex reviews under deadlines in a fast-paced environment
Willingness to work in an on-site or hybrid setup in Vienna (depending on business needs) and to complete a background check where required
responsibilities: We are looking for an experienced Senior Due Diligence Specialist to join Bitget EU's Anti-Financial Crime (AFC) / Compliance team in Vienna. In this senior contributor role, you will perform and oversee complex KYC/EDD reviews for retail customers.

responsibilities: Responsible for the construction of risk control models in various risk domains, including but not limited to:

jobs.description:
About us

Bitget Wallet is the non-custodial crypto wallet designed to make crypto simple and secure for everyone. With over 80 million users, we bring together a full suite of crypto services, including swaps, market insights, staking, rewards, DApp exploration, and payment solutions. Design and develop the core backend modules of our DEX, including:• Swap (AMM) matching, single-chain & cross-chain transactions, and limit-order matching engine• In-house aggregator for routing, quotation, and optimal path computation
Help architect and implement backend services that meet high-availability, high-concurrency, and high-scalability standards.
Integrate deeply with EVM/Solana smart contracts: build on-chain interaction logic, listen to and parse on-chain events, and ensure data consistency between on-chain and off-chain systems.
Optimize the performance and security of the trading pipeline; continuously monitor and improve TPS, latency, fault tolerance, and risk-control capability.
Collaborate closely with product, frontend, smart-contract, and DevOps teams to drive rapid iteration and deliver high-quality products.
Participate in code reviews, technical sharing, documentation, and best-practice development
requirements: Bachelor’s degree or above in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
3+ years of backend development experience, with 1+ years of hands-on Golang; familiar with Gin, gRPC, Etcd, etc.
At least one project involving Dapp or smart-contract interaction on EVM or Solana; deep understanding of contract invocation, transaction lifecycle, nonce & gas mechanisms.
Solid knowledge of AMM principles (e.g., Uniswap v2/v3, Curve, Balancer) and common aggregator path-optimization algorithms.

Proficient in backend fundamentals such as microservices, message queues, caching, and databases:• Middleware: Redis/Memcache, Kafka/RabbitMQ• Databases: MySQL/PostgreSQL plus at least one NoSQL (MongoDB/ClickHouse, etc.)• Container & DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD
Strong grasp of data structures, algorithms, and concurrent programming; able to diagnose performance bottlenecks in high-concurrency scenarios independently.
Excellent system design, documentation, communication, and self-learning skills.
